title: Aerangis modesta
intro: Aerangis modesta, also known as the 'modest Aerangis', is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Aerangis modesta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Aerangis modesta is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Aerangis, which consists of about 40 species of orchids. The genus Aerangis is part of the subtribe Aerangidinae, within the tribe Vandeae. The subtribe Aerangidinae is characterized by its unique flower structure and is primarily found in tropical Africa. Aerangis modesta is a relatively small orchid species native to tropical regions of Africa.
